brand.tagline: Clarity for families navigating German long-term care

common.estimate: This is an estimate, not an official assessment.

home.hero_title: Most families receive a lower Pflegegrad than they are entitled to. Every month that goes unchallenged costs €300–600.
home.hero_sub: Klara helps caregivers in Germany prepare for the MDK assessment so the grade you receive matches the care you actually give. Plain-language explanations, a 14-day care diary, and a 5-minute check.
home.hero_calc_body: The difference between Pflegegrad 2 and Pflegegrad 3 is
home.hero_calc_note: Cash benefit (Pflegegeld) alone — before counting Pflegesachleistung, Entlastungsbetrag or residential top-ups.
home.trust_title: Built around what the assessor actually scores
home.trust_body: The Neues Begutachtungsassessment (NBA) under SGB XI §15 doesn't reward minimised answers. It rewards honest, specific descriptions of daily life. Klara helps you give those answers.
home.features_title: Everything you need before the visit
home.feat1_body: 10 simple questions across the 6 NBA modules give you an estimated Pflegegrad in under 5 minutes.
home.feat2_body: Two templates — one for autism, one for elderly care — designed around what the assessor actually scores.
home.feat3_body: Every official MDK question with a plain-language paraphrase telling you what the assessor is really asking.
home.feat4_body: Turn 14 days of diary entries into a one-page summary you can hand to the assessor.
home.for_who_body: Klara is written for caregivers — the family member or close friend doing the day-to-day care. The questions and prompts always speak to you.
home.autism_body: Standard care diaries miss what matters most: prompting, supervision, behavioural regulation, and safety. Klara's autism diary captures all of it.
home.elderly_body: Mobility, orientation, falls, night-time care, medication supervision — Klara's elderly diary mirrors exactly what Module 1–6 score.

modules.title: The 6 NBA modules
modules.sub: The Pflegegrad is calculated from six weighted modules. Here is what each one really measures.
modules.m1_body: Can the person change position, sit up, stand, walk and use stairs without physical help? Motor only — cognitive limitations aren't scored here. Weighted to 10 points.
modules.m2_body: Recognising people, orientation in time and place, recalling events, making everyday decisions, holding a conversation. Weighted to 15 points.
modules.m3_body: Frequency of meltdowns, aggression, anxiety, refusing help, night-time disturbance, withdrawal. Combined with Module 2 — the higher of the two scores counts.
modules.m4_body: Washing, dressing, toileting, eating, drinking. Eating is weighted triple. The most heavily weighted module — up to 40 points.
modules.m5_body: Medication, injections, monitoring, doctor visits, therapy, diet. Counts how many measures per day, week or month require support. Weighted to 20 points.
modules.m6_body: Structuring the day, planning activities, social contact in and outside the home. Weighted to 15 points.

how.title: How Klara works
how.sub: From your first application date to the assessor's visit — in four steps.
how.s1_body: Answer 10 plain-language questions to see the Pflegegrad your relative is likely to receive. Free, no signup.
how.s2_body: Fill in one page per day for 14 days. The diary captures the patterns the assessor scores — not just time spent.
how.s3_body: Every official MDK question shown with a caregiver-friendly explanation of what the assessor is really asking.
how.s4_body: Klara turns your diary into a one-page summary. Bring it to the assessment visit.
how.basics_p1: A Pflegegrad (care grade) is the level of long-term care need assigned under German social law (SGB XI §15). It determines which benefits a person and their family receive from the statutory long-term care insurance — cash allowances, home-care services, day care, residential care, and aids.
how.basics_p2: The grade is decided after an assessment by the Medizinischer Dienst (MD/MDK), using the Neues Begutachtungsassessment (NBA). The assessor scores how independent the person is across six life domains, weights the scores, and adds them up. The total — from 0 to 100 points — maps to one of five grades.
how.grades_sub: Higher grade = more support needs recognised, and more benefits unlocked.

grade.g1_implies: Some help is needed in daily life, but the person is largely self-reliant. Access to counselling and minor relief services.
grade.g2_implies: Regular daily support needed. Eligible for care allowance (Pflegegeld), home-care services, day care, and respite care.
grade.g3_implies: Substantial support across most areas of daily life. Higher benefits, eligibility for residential or intensive home care.
grade.g4_implies: Continuous, complex care needs. Full access to inpatient care benefits and the highest tier of home-care support.
grade.g5_label: Most severe impairment with special demands
grade.g5_implies: Maximum care needs — typically continuous supervision, complete dependence in self-care, or specialised medical needs. Highest benefit level.

how.benefits_note: Cash benefit (Pflegegeld) is paid to family caregivers. Care-service amounts (Pflegesachleistung) apply when professional services are used instead. Figures are official 2025 rates.
how.benefits_table_sub: What recipients and their families can claim from the long-term care insurance at each grade (2025 rates, in €).
how.benefits_table_note: In addition, all grades receive up to €42/month for care aids and up to €4,000 one-time for home modifications. Cash and service benefits can be combined (Kombinationsleistung).
how.mods_sub: Each module is scored, then weighted into the final total.
how.mods_note: * Modules 2 and 3 share a single weighted contribution — only the higher of the two counts.

mod.m1_body: Changing position, sitting, standing, walking, stairs.
mod.m4_body: Washing, dressing, toileting, eating (×3 weighted), drinking.
mod.m5_body: Medication, injections, doctor visits, therapy, diet.
mod.m6_body: Structuring the day, planning activities, social contact.

how.process_sub: From the first application to the official decision.

process.p1_d: A short written or phone application — benefits start from the application date.
process.p2_d: Usually within a few weeks. You'll receive a date by post.
process.p3_d: Run the quick check, fill the 14-day diary, work through the full assessment. Generate your AI report.
process.p4_d: The assessor observes, asks questions across all 6 modules, and takes notes.
process.p5_d: Raw scores per module → weighted points → total out of 100.
process.p6_d: The Pflegekasse issues the Pflegegrad in writing, typically within 25 working days.
process.p7_d: You have 1 month to formally object. A new assessment can be requested.

intro.title: A quick tour before you start
intro.sub: Three tools work together to prepare you for the MDK visit.
intro.s1_body: Answer 10 plain-language questions to see the Pflegegrad your relative is likely to receive. No signup.
intro.s2_body: Log a short page each day. The diary captures the patterns the assessor scores — not just hours of help.
intro.s3_body: Work through every official MDK question, then generate a one-page AI report (€9) to bring to the visit.

survey.title: 5-minute Pflegegrad check
survey.sub: 10 plain-language questions. We'll estimate the care grade your relative is likely to receive.
survey.disclaimer: This is an estimate, not an official assessment. Only the MDK / Medizinischer Dienst can assign a Pflegegrad.
survey.who_autism: Someone with autism or a developmental condition

q.mobility_help: On your hardest days, without your physical help.
q.cognition: Knowing where they are, what day it is, recognising people
q.cognition_help: When things are at their most difficult, without prompting from you.
q.behaviour: Meltdowns, agitation, refusing care or night-time disturbance
q.behaviour_help: On your hardest days, roughly how often.
q.hygiene_help: On your hardest days, without prompting or physical help.
q.dressing_help: When things are at their most difficult, without you choosing or helping.
q.eating_help: On your hardest days, without reminders or help.
q.toilet_help: On your hardest days — including continence and clean-up.

survey.halfway: You're halfway there. Keep going — the next answers matter most.
survey.answer_required: Please choose an answer to continue.
survey.autosave_hint: Your answers save automatically on this device.
survey.result_none: Below the threshold for a care grade
survey.result_reveal_label: Based on your responses, your relative shows indicators consistent with
survey.result_gap_title: The difference between your current grade and this prediction
survey.result_gap_note: Estimated gap if currently graded one level lower. Pflegegeld figures only.
survey.result_explain: Based on your answers, your relative is likely to receive {{label}}. The official MDK assessment looks at many more details — Klara's full assessment walks you through every question.
survey.result_next: Prepare in detail with Klara — €9 per report
survey.result_next_frame: €9 once vs. up to €{{m}} every month you go undergraded.
survey.age_title: Who are you completing this for?
survey.age_child_sub: Child-specific MDK questions used for under-18 assessments.

child.title: Pflegegrad check — children & young people
child.sub: 10 plain-language questions adapted for assessing a child under 18.
child.under3_hint: Some self-care items (washing, dressing, toileting) are not scored for children under 3 because full independence is not age-typical.
child.halfway: You're halfway there. The next answers — about your child's self-care — carry the most weight.
child.result_reveal_label: Based on your responses, your child shows indicators consistent with

q.child_mobility: Moving around inside the home compared to other children the same age
q.child_mobility_help: On your hardest days, without your physical help. Compare to what is typical for the child's age.
q.child_cognition: Understanding instructions, recognising people and routines
q.child_cognition_help: When things are at their most difficult, compared to age-typical development.
q.child_behaviour: Meltdowns, aggression, self-injury, night-time disturbance, refusing care
q.child_behaviour_help: On your hardest days, roughly how often you must intervene.
q.child_hygiene_help: On your hardest days, without prompting or physical help — beyond what is age-typical.
q.child_dressing_help: When things are at their most difficult, without you choosing or helping — beyond age-typical needs.
q.child_eating_help: On your hardest days, without reminders, cutting, or feeding help beyond age-typical.
q.child_toilet: Using the toilet (or managing pads / incontinence)
q.child_toilet_help: On your hardest days — including continence and clean-up, beyond age-typical.
q.child_therapy: Managing therapy, medication, doctor and clinic appointments
q.child_therapy_help: How much you must prepare, administer, monitor, and accompany.
q.child_structure: Structuring the day — school, kindergarten, activities, transitions
q.child_structure_help: Without your constant prompting or planning.
q.child_social: Social contact and play with other children
q.child_social_help: Compared to age-typical participation — can they manage without your intervention?

assessment.title: Full MDK assessment
assessment.intro: The same structure the Medizinischer Dienst uses, with plain-language paraphrases and honest-answer prompts for caregivers. Your answers are saved on this device.
assessment.alert_body: Not their best day. Not their worst. What is normal across day and night? The assessor rewards specific, honest descriptions — not minimised answers.
assessment.self_estimate: This is a self-completed estimate, not an official assessment. The MDK visit will review the same criteria with an in-person observation.
assessment.clear_confirm: Clear all answers on this device?
assessment.notes_label: Notes for the assessor (optional but recommended)
assessment.notes_placeholder: A short, specific example helps a lot. e.g. 'Refuses morning wash 4/7 days, takes 30 min to redirect.'

diary.pro_badge: Pro feature · saving locally for now
diary.title: Care diary
diary.intro: One short page per day. Aim for 14 consecutive days before your MDK visit — Klara will turn the entries into a one-page summary you can hand to the assessor.
diary.honest_body: The assessor rewards specific descriptions of how much help is actually needed — prompting, supervision and emotional regulation all count. Score a typical day, not your best day.
diary.enough_entries: You have enough entries for a strong report.
diary.tip: Tip: 14 days gives the assessor the clearest picture, but you can generate a draft any time.
diary.entries_desc: Click an entry to load it for editing.
diary.no_entries: No entries yet. Fill in today above and click Save.
diary.note_placeholder: A short note in your own words…

report.title: MDK preparation report
report.intro: Klara reads your full assessment answers and your care diary, then drafts a one-page report you can print and bring to the assessor.
report.inputs_desc: Reports are generated from what you have already entered in the assessment and diary. Nothing is uploaded until you sign in.
report.nothing_body_1: Fill in at least a few items in the
report.error_generic: Something went wrong generating the report.
report.draft_desc: Review and edit anything that doesn't match your day-to-day reality before printing.
